front: Poem: speaker apologizes for eating "the plums / that were in / the icebox" — "they were delicious / so sweet / and so cold"
back: "This Is Just to Say" (William Carlos Williams)

front: Poem: Kenneth Koch parodied this poem in "Variations on a Theme by" its author — speaker chops down a house and sprays hollyhocks with lye
back: "This Is Just to Say" (William Carlos Williams)

front: Poem: "so much depends / upon / a red wheel / barrow / glazed with rain / water / beside the white / chickens"
Poem: epic about a city "under the Passaic Falls" in New Jersey; repeats "no ideas but in things" beginning in "The Delineaments of the Giants"
Poem: title poem begins "By the road to the contagious hospital"; collection also contains "The pure products of America go crazy" and "The Red Wheelbarrow"
Poem: love poem in triadic-line form; title flower is "like a buttercup / upon its branching stem"; declares "All women are not Helen, I know that, but have Helen in their hearts"
Poem: "it is difficult / to get the news from poems / yet men die miserably every day / for lack / of what is found there"
Author: wrote the introduction to Ginsberg's Howl — "Hold back the edges of your gowns, Ladies, we are going through hell"
Author: "I saw the figure 5 in gold" from "The Great Figure" inspired a painting by Charles Demuth
Author: physician (obstetrician) from Rutherford, NJ; invented the triadic line; wrote In the American Grain arguing Burr was only Founder to see women as equals
